title: [Bug] `podman-load` does not exit with an error code if unsuccessful
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman-load` does not exit with an error code if loading the image was unsuccessful due to insufficient disk space. This is especially  problematic when the `podman-load` command is part of an automated deployment (i.e. Ansible), where it is not clear immediately that loading the image failed.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Dump an image with `podman save --output dump.tar`

2. Try to load the image on a machine with insufficient disk space with `podman load --input dump.tar`

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman` tries to load the image, and prints an output as follows:

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2c9f341968bc skipped: already exists  
Copying blob ad6562704f37 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob c8c689912329 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 10330b33cf48 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 3bf6b9a7f67b skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 2c1b03a92761 [=======>------------------------------] 160.0MiB / 752.9MiB
Copying blob 9a262f6537f2 done  
Copying blob 5856d60a6abb done  
Copying blob fb276b370568 done  
Copying blob 2c5197e86b95 [================================>-----] 158.3MiB / 180.9MiB
Copying blob 45fe96b96aba done  
Copying blob 69515dc82e72 done  
Copying blob a0e517e8a5f2 done  
Copying blob ba62588f42c7 done  
Copying blob 6ea6a3bf06fe done  
Copying blob c8cc25f958b6 done  
Copying blob 023a323af376 done  
Copying blob f4e4801bb8c2 done  
Copying blob db6dad77783e done  
Copying blob 2e9e1996c1fc done  
  write /var/tmp/storage120040653/1: no space left on device
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2c9f341968bc skipped: already exists  
Copying blob ad6562704f37 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 3bf6b9a7f67b skipped: already exists  
Copying blob c8c689912329 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 10330b33cf48 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 2c1b03a92761 [=======>------------------------------] 154.1MiB / 752.9MiB
Copying blob 9a262f6537f2 done  
Copying blob fb276b370568 done  
Copying blob 2c5197e86b95 [==================================>---] 166.3MiB / 180.9MiB
Copying blob 45fe96b96aba done  
Copying blob 5856d60a6abb done  
Copying blob 69515dc82e72 done  
Copying blob ba62588f42c7 done  
Copying blob 6ea6a3bf06fe done  
Copying blob a0e517e8a5f2 done  
Copying blob c8cc25f958b6 done  
Copying blob 023a323af376 done  
Copying blob f4e4801bb8c2 done  
Copying blob db6dad77783e done  
Copying blob 2e9e1996c1fc done  
  write /var/tmp/storage421829064/1: no space left on device
  open ansible.detely7h.application-image.tar/manifest.json: not a directory
  open ansible.detely7h.application-image.tar/index.json: not a directory
Loaded image(s):
```

An `echo $?` prints `0` as if there was no error.

**Describe the results you expected:**

In case of an error, I expect `podman` to return an error code. So `echo $?` should print `1`, as `docker` does on the same case.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.15
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

VMware ESXi

comments.body: Version v3.0.1 is very old, please try with the latest version v.4.1.1 or at least v4.0. It is very likely that this was already fixed.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Sine I got no feedback I assume this was fixed already in a later version
